steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows 'is largely ______ of the fact that' and must describe how the speed difference relates to the reason given. The phrase 'of the fact that' signals a causal relationship, so the blank needs a word that frames the speed gap as something that follows from that reason.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — a consequence: 'a consequence of the fact' means the speed difference results from the fact about flight features — a perfect causal fit. ✓
  • Choice B — a repudiation: repudiation means denial or rejection. The text does not argue against the fact; it presents it as an explanation. ✗
  • Choice C — an objective: objective implies a goal. A speed difference is not something the animals are aiming for; it is an observed result. ✗
  • Choice D — an explanation: the speed difference cannot explain the fact that flight features limit speeds less — that reverses the causal direction. The fact explains the difference, not the other way around. ✗
That matches Choice A — a consequence.
